reasoning:

For the first resolution date (2025-11-16), resolving to the published value available at that time, it will most likely be the November 5 value (or a slightly revised October 29/November 12 one). Since 1057.27 is distinctly higher than the October 29 value (1056.09), there is a strong probability (75%) that the series will have increased.
For the second resolution date (2025-12-09), relying on recent upward momentum (slope of +0.08 per day) and the trend through Q4 holiday-season lending, it is probable to remain elevated relative to November 9. However, there is added horizon uncertainty, projecting a 64% likelihood.
For the third resolution date (2026-02-07), historical reference (e.g., severe drop in January 2025 from ~1076 to ~1040) points to non-trivial chances of structure breaks or post-holiday drawdowns not fully captured by seasonal adjustments. Extended time horizons further justify shifting closer to the baseline probability of 0.5. I estimate a 52% chance it remains higher by February.
